reason: Disabling CAN support is a standard hardening measure that involves a simple configuration change to blacklist an unnecessary kernel module. It is unlikely to cause lockout or break critical services in a typical server environment, as CAN is not commonly used outside specialized industrial or automotive contexts. The remediation follows a straightforward pattern similar to other STIG-approved module disablements.
estimated_impact: Potential breakage if CAN-dependent applications are running in specialized environments
